Rear seat fold-down: If you frequently fold the rear seat for cargo, make sure the covers allow this without removal. Good covers have flaps or openings for the latch mechanisms.

MOLLE compatibility: If you use your TJ for overlanding or tactical purposes, consider covers with built-in MOLLE webbing. This lets you attach MOLLE pouches, accessories, and gear bags directly to your seats — adding organized storage without permanent modifications.

Color and Style Options

The most popular TJ seat cover colors:

  • Black: Hides dirt, goes with everything, most popular choice
  • Black/Graphite: Subtle two-tone that adds character without being flashy
  • Coyote/Khaki: Tactical look, popular with the military/overlanding crowd
  • Black/Red or Black/Blue: For TJ owners who want some personality

Protecting the Whole Interior

Seat covers are just the start. For complete TJ interior protection, consider:

  • Grab handles: Paracord grab handles replace worn factory handles and add a custom look
  • Sun visor organizers: Keep essentials within reach
  • MOLLE panels: Mount on the back of front seats for rear passenger storage

Bottom Line

Your TJ deserves better than ripped factory seats or cheap universal covers. Invest in custom-fit, tactical-grade seat covers made from real CORDURA® fabric, and your Wrangler's interior will look better than it did when it rolled off the line.

Browse our complete selection of Jeep Wrangler seat covers — we have options for TJ, JK, and JL models in multiple colors and configurations.

articles.title: MOLLE Panel Organizers for Vehicles: The Military-Grade Storage System Your Rig Needs
articles.body_html:
What Is MOLLE and Why Is It Showing Up in Vehicles?

If you've spent any time around military gear, tactical equipment, or even hiking backpacks, you've probably seen MOLLE webbing — those neat rows of stitched nylon loops that let you attach pouches, tools, and accessories in a modular grid pattern. MOLLE stands for Modular Lightweight Load-carrying Equipment, and it's been the standard attachment system for U.S. military load-bearing gear since the late 1990s.

So what does military gear have to do with your daily driver or weekend trail rig? Everything, it turns out.

Vehicle owners — especially in the Jeep, Bronco, and overlanding communities — have discovered that MOLLE panels solve one of the most frustrating problems in any vehicle: how to organize gear in a space that wasn't designed for it. Instead of throwing everything in a pile on the back seat or cramming it into a center console, MOLLE lets you mount individual pouches and holders exactly where you need them, and rearrange the layout whenever your needs change.

How MOLLE Vehicle Organizers Work

The concept is simple and that's exactly why it works so well. A MOLLE system has two components:

  • The panel: A flat surface covered in horizontal rows of webbing (the MOLLE grid). In vehicles, these panels mount to sun visors, headrests, seat backs, tailgates, roll bars, or overhead areas.
  • The attachments: Pouches, holsters, straps, and holders that weave through the MOLLE webbing and lock into place. They stay put over rough terrain but can be repositioned in seconds when you want to change things up.

There are no screws, no permanent modifications, no adhesive that fails in the heat. MOLLE attachments are held by friction and gravity through interlocking webbing straps — the harder you pull down, the tighter they grip. It's brilliantly simple engineering that's been battle-tested for decades.

Where to Mount MOLLE Panels in Your Vehicle

The beauty of a MOLLE-based system is flexibility. Here are the most popular mounting locations and what works best in each spot:

Sun Visors

Your sun visors are sitting right there doing almost nothing most of the time. A MOLLE visor cover transforms them into prime storage real estate. Mount a slim pouch for your registration and insurance documents, a pen holder, sunglasses case, or a small first aid kit. Everything stays overhead and out of the way but within arm's reach.

Bartact makes MOLLE visor covers that slip over your existing visor with an elastic wrap — no modification needed. They're built with the same 1000D Cordura nylon used across the entire product line, so they won't sag, stretch, or fall apart.

Seat Backs

The back of your front seats is another underused space. A MOLLE seat-back panel gives rear passengers access to pouches for water bottles, tablets, snacks, maps, or trail guides. For off-road rigs, this is a great spot for a first aid kit or a radio pouch — visible and accessible from the back seat without anyone needing to dig through a bag.

Roll Bars and Overhead

Jeep Wrangler and Ford Bronco owners have roll bars running the length of the cabin — that's a massive amount of mounting surface going to waste. MOLLE roll bar covers wrap around the bars, adding protective padding while giving you a 360-degree attachment surface for pouches, flashlights, and small gear. Pair these with overhead sun shades that include MOLLE panels, and you've essentially created an overhead storage shelf out of thin air.

Tailgate and Cargo Area

For overlanders and trail rigs, the tailgate and cargo area are the hub of your gear setup. MOLLE panels mounted to the tailgate interior give you organized storage that's immediately accessible when you swing the gate open — tools, recovery gear, cleaning supplies, first aid, whatever your trip demands.

What to Look for in Quality MOLLE Gear

Not all MOLLE products are created equal. The market has been flooded with cheap knockoffs that use thin webbing, weak stitching, and materials that break down quickly — especially in a vehicle environment where they're exposed to heat, UV radiation, and vibration. Here's what separates the good stuff from the junk:

Material: 1000D Cordura Nylon

This is the gold standard. 1000-denier Cordura nylon is what the military specifies for load-bearing equipment because it resists abrasion, tearing, and punctures. Cheaper products often use 600D or even 400D fabric — they feel similar when new but won't hold up under real use. All of Bartact's MOLLE pouches and accessories use genuine 1000D Cordura.

UV Protection

This is the one most people forget about. Your vehicle interior gets blasted with UV radiation — especially if you're running a Jeep or Bronco with the top off. Non-UV-protected nylon fades from black to a washed-out gray in a single summer. Bartact uses UV-protected polyester thread and UV-resistant materials throughout, so your MOLLE setup looks as good in year three as it did on day one.

Stitching Quality

Every MOLLE row is a line of stitching, and every line is a potential failure point. Look for double-stitched or bar-tacked stress points. Bartact's products are sewn in their Southern California facility where each piece gets inspected by hand. That's a level of quality control that mass-produced imports can't match — and it's why their MOLLE gear carries a Berry Compliant certification, meeting U.S. military procurement standards for materials and workmanship.

Correct MOLLE Spacing

True MOLLE webbing follows a specific standard for row height and spacing. This matters because your attachments need to weave through properly. Non-standard spacing means pouches don't lock in correctly and can shift or fall off during driving. Stick with manufacturers who build to actual military specifications.

Best MOLLE Accessories for Vehicle Use

Once you've got your panels installed, the fun part begins — outfitting them. Here are the most useful MOLLE vehicle accessories based on how people actually use their rigs:

General Purpose Pouches

The workhorse of any MOLLE setup. A medium-sized zippered pouch is the right size for phone chargers, multi-tools, flashlights, snacks, or a compact first aid kit. Get at least two — one for the driver's visor and one on a roll bar or seat back.

Water Bottle/Radio Holders

Elastic-sided holders that grip a Nalgene bottle or handheld radio. Mount these on a roll bar cover or seat-back panel for easy grab-and-go access.

Admin/Document Pouches

A flat pouch with internal organization for pens, papers, cards, and small documents. Perfect for the sun visor — keeps your registration, trail maps, and park passes organized and accessible.

MOLLE Buckles and Attachment Straps

Don't overlook the small hardware. MOLLE buckles and attachment accessories let you strap down irregularly shaped items, create custom lashing points, or connect two MOLLE surfaces together. They're cheap, versatile, and save you from having to buy a specialized pouch for every single item.

MOLLE Storage Strips

For flat surfaces where a full panel is overkill, MOLLE storage strips give you a single row or two of attachment points. Great for inside tailgates, behind seats, or along the inside of a truck bed wall.

Vehicle-Specific MOLLE Setups

Different vehicles have different mounting opportunities. Here's a quick breakdown:

Jeep Wrangler (JK, JL, JLU)

The Wrangler is the king of MOLLE-friendly vehicles thanks to its exposed roll bars, removable panels, and active aftermarket community. Start with visor covers and roll bar covers, then add overhead sun shade panels with MOLLE for maximum storage. Browse the full Jeep Wrangler accessories collection for model-specific options.

Ford Bronco (2021+)

The full-size Bronco has a similar open-top design philosophy to the Wrangler, making it perfect for MOLLE accessories. Sun shades, visor covers, and roll bar organizers all work great. Check the Ford Bronco accessories collection for options designed specifically for the Bronco's interior dimensions.

Toyota Tacoma and 4Runner

While these don't have the exposed roll bars of a Wrangler or Bronco, visor covers and seat-back organizers work beautifully. The Tacoma's limited cab space actually makes MOLLE organization even more valuable — every square inch counts. See Toyota 4Runner accessories and Toyota Tacoma accessories for vehicle-specific options.

How to Plan Your MOLLE Layout

Before you buy everything at once, take a step back and think about how you actually use your vehicle:

  1. List your gear: What do you carry regularly? First aid, tools, recovery gear, electronics, snacks, documents?
  2. Prioritize by access: What do you need while driving vs. what you grab when stopped? Driving essentials go on visors and the center area. Stop-and-grab gear goes on seat backs and the tailgate.
  3. Start small: Begin with visor covers and a couple of pouches. Live with the setup for a few weeks, then add more as you figure out what you actually need within reach.
  4. Leave room to grow: One of MOLLE's best features is that you can always add more later. Don't fill every row on day one.

The Bottom Line

MOLLE panel organizers bring a level of modular, customizable storage to your vehicle that no other system can match. The military figured this out decades ago — it just took the automotive aftermarket a while to catch up.

When you're choosing MOLLE gear for your vehicle, quality matters more than quantity. Start with panels and pouches built from genuine 1000D Cordura nylon with UV protection, proper MOLLE spacing, and quality stitching. Products made in the USA to military specifications will outlast cheap alternatives many times over.

Explore the full Bartact MOLLE collection to see panels, pouches, strips, and accessories built to handle whatever you throw at them — on the trail or on the daily commute.

articles.title: Ford Bronco Storage Solutions for 2026: How to Organize Your Bronco's Interior
articles.body_html:
The Ford Bronco's Storage Problem (and How to Fix It)

The Ford Bronco is one of the most exciting vehicles on the road right now. Whether you picked up a 2021 first-edition or you're rolling in a 2025 model, there's nothing quite like pulling the top off a Bronco and hitting the trail — or honestly, just cruising to the grocery store feeling like an absolute boss.

But here's the thing every Bronco owner figures out pretty quickly: interior storage is... limited. Ford designed the Bronco for adventure, not for hauling file cabinets. The center console is small. The door pockets are shallow. The cargo area behind the rear seats is workable but disorganized. And once you start adding trail gear, recovery equipment, tools, and all the stuff that comes with actually using your Bronco the way it was meant to be used, things get chaotic fast.

The good news? The aftermarket has caught up. There are smart, purpose-built storage solutions that work with the Bronco's unique design instead of against it. Let's break down the best ways to organize your Bronco heading into 2026.

MOLLE Panels: The Modular Storage System Bronco Owners Love

If you're not familiar with MOLLE (Modular Lightweight Load-carrying Equipment), it's a grid-based attachment system originally developed for the U.S. military. Rows of heavy-duty nylon webbing create a universal mounting surface where you can clip on pouches, holders, straps, and tools — then rearrange everything whenever you want.

For the Ford Bronco, MOLLE panels are practically tailor-made. The Bronco's removable top and open-air design mean the interior gets exposed to sun, dust, and weather. A MOLLE system built from quality materials handles all of that without fading, sagging, or falling apart.

Here's where MOLLE panels work best in the Bronco:

Sun Visor MOLLE Covers

Your Bronco's sun visors are prime real estate that's going to waste. MOLLE visor covers slip over the existing visor and instantly give you a modular attachment surface right above your head. Mount a slim document pouch for your registration and insurance, a sunglasses holder, a pen organizer, or a compact first aid kit. Everything stays overhead and accessible without cluttering your console or door pockets.

The key is getting visor covers made from durable materials that can handle the UV exposure Bronco interiors get — especially when the top is off. Bartact's visor covers use 1000D Cordura nylon and UV-protected polyester, the same materials specified for military equipment. They won't fade to gray after one summer in the Arizona sun.

Roll Bar and Overhead MOLLE Panels

The Bronco's roll bar structure runs through the cabin and provides an incredible amount of potential mounting surface. With MOLLE roll bar covers, you wrap the bars in protective padding that doubles as a storage grid. Clip on water bottle holders, flashlight pouches, radio holsters, or general-purpose organizers — all within arm's reach from the driver or passenger seat.

Combine roll bar covers with overhead sun shades that include MOLLE panels, and you've essentially built yourself an organized overhead shelf. Sun protection plus storage in a single product. It's the kind of practical upgrade that makes you wonder why it isn't factory standard.

Seat-Back Organizers

The back of your front seats is another overlooked storage zone. A MOLLE panel mounted to the seat back gives rear passengers organized, accessible storage for water bottles, tablets, snacks, trail maps, and tools. This is especially valuable on the 4-door Bronco where rear passengers need their own gear access.

Seat Covers with Purpose: Protection Plus Organization

Speaking of seats — let's talk about one of the smartest dual-purpose upgrades for the Bronco: tactical seat covers.

The Bronco's seats get hammered. Top off, doors off, trail dust everywhere, wet swimsuits after a lake day, dog claws, muddy boots — factory upholstery was not designed for this level of abuse. You need seat covers. But not all seat covers are created equal, and for a Bronco, you need covers that can take a beating while actually adding functionality.

Bartact's Ford Bronco seat covers are made in Southern California from UV-protected polyester combined with 1000D Cordura nylon, with foam and scrim backing for a factory-fit feel. They're designed specifically for the Bronco's seat dimensions — no universal-fit guessing games where the cover bunches up or exposes the corners.

Several styles include integrated MOLLE panels on the seat backs, which means your seat covers are simultaneously protecting your upholstery AND providing organized storage. That's the kind of two-for-one upgrade that makes a real difference in a vehicle with limited interior space.

The materials matter here more than most people realize. The Bronco is an open-air vehicle. Seat covers that aren't UV-protected will fade within months. Covers without water-resistant backing will trap moisture against your factory seats. And cheap stitching will fail under the constant stress of people climbing in and out of a vehicle with a higher-than-average step-in height.

Bartact's Berry Compliant manufacturing means every aspect — from the thread to the fabric to the foam — meets U.S. military procurement standards. When a product is built to survive military field use, your weekend trail run isn't going to faze it.

Grab Handles: Access and Storage in One

The Bronco sits high, and if you've lifted yours, it sits even higher. Getting in and out — especially for passengers — calls for proper grab handles. But beyond just helping people climb aboard, grab handles serve as anchor points for small MOLLE pouches, carabiners, and attachment loops.

Bartact's paracord grab handles for the Ford Bronco are wrapped in genuine 550 paracord, available in tons of color combos, and they actually provide a comfortable, secure grip — a big step up from the factory plastic. In a pinch, that paracord is rated to 550 pounds, making it functional survival gear as well.

Mount them on the A-pillar and roll bar for full cabin coverage. They're a quick install with no tools required.

Cargo Area Organization

The Bronco's cargo area has decent volume but minimal built-in organization. Everything you put back there slides around during driving, which means your carefully packed trail gear ends up in a pile by the time you reach the trailhead.

Here are the best approaches to taming the cargo area:

Tailgate MOLLE Panels

The inside of the tailgate (or swing gate, depending on your configuration) is a flat, accessible surface that's perfectly suited for a MOLLE panel. Mount your most frequently needed items here — a first aid kit, a flashlight, recovery gear straps, or a multi-tool pouch. When you swing the gate open, everything is organized and ready.

Cargo Tie-Downs

Sometimes the simplest solution is the best one. Quality cargo tie-downs keep your gear from sliding around without requiring a complex storage system. Pair them with small gear bags to create organized zones within the cargo area.

Gear Bags and Duffels

For larger gear organization, tactical bags and backpacks with external MOLLE webbing let you organize gear into categories (tools, recovery, first aid, personal items) while the bags themselves can attach to MOLLE panels in the cargo area. It creates a system where everything has a home.

Fire Safety: Don't Skip This One

This isn't the most glamorous storage solution, but it might be the most important one. If you take your Bronco off-road — or through dry brush areas, or near campfires — having a fire extinguisher within reach isn't optional. It's essential.

The problem is where to put it. You don't want a fire extinguisher rattling around loose in your cargo area, and you definitely don't want it buried under a pile of camping gear when you actually need it.

A roll bar-mounted fire extinguisher holder keeps it secure, silent, and instantly accessible from the driver's seat. Bartact's mounts work with the Bronco's roll bar diameter and hold the extinguisher firmly even over the roughest terrain. It's a five-minute install that could genuinely save your vehicle — or your life.

Why Material Quality Matters More in a Bronco

Here's something worth emphasizing: the Ford Bronco is harder on accessories than most vehicles. The combination of UV exposure (top off/panels removed), dust intrusion (doors off), temperature extremes, and the vibration of off-road use means that cheaply made accessories fail faster in a Bronco than they would in a sedan.

This is why material specs matter. 1000D Cordura nylon is the benchmark for durability in tactical and military applications. UV-protected polyester thread won't degrade in direct sunlight. Foam and scrim backing maintains its shape instead of compressing into nothing. And products made in the USA — specifically, Bartact's facility in Southern California — get the quality control that ensures consistency from one piece to the next.

The Berry Compliant certification is worth paying attention to here. It means the entire product — materials and manufacturing — meets the standards required for U.S. military procurement. These aren't just marketing terms; they're measurable quality benchmarks that directly affect how long your accessories last.

Building Your Bronco Storage System: A Practical Starting Point

If you're starting from zero, here's a phased approach that won't overwhelm your wallet or your weekend:

  1. Phase 1 — Protect and Organize the Basics: Start with seat covers and visor covers. These two upgrades protect your interior while adding immediate storage. Total install time: under an hour.
  2. Phase 2 — Add Cabin Storage: Install roll bar covers and a few MOLLE pouches. Add grab handles. This gives you organized, accessible storage throughout the cabin.
  3. Phase 3 — Complete the System: Add cargo area organization with tie-downs, gear bags, and a fire extinguisher mount. Dial in your MOLLE pouch layout based on how you've been using the vehicle.

The beauty of a modular system like MOLLE is that you can build it incrementally. Start with what you need most and expand over time.

Explore the Full Ford Bronco Collection

Ready to get your Bronco organized? Browse the complete Ford Bronco Accessories collection to see seat covers, MOLLE gear, grab handles, sun shades, fire safety equipment, and everything else designed specifically for the 2021+ Bronco.

Every product is made in Southern California, backed by military-spec materials, and designed to handle the unique demands of Bronco ownership. Because your Bronco was built for adventure — your accessories should be too.

Have questions about what fits your specific Bronco model? Contact the Bartact team and we'll get you sorted out.

articles.title: Best Jeep Wrangler Accessories for 2026: The Upgrades That Actually Matter
articles.body_html:
Why 2026 Is a Great Year to Upgrade Your Jeep Wrangler

If you own a Jeep Wrangler — whether it's a classic TJ, a trusty JK, or a current-gen JL — you already know the aftermarket world is massive. There are thousands of accessories promising to make your rig better. The problem? Most of them are cheaply made overseas, fade after a season in the sun, and end up in a box in your garage.

We've put together this guide to cut through the noise. These are the Jeep Wrangler accessories that actually hold up to trail abuse, daily driving, and everything in between heading into 2026. No gimmicks — just upgrades that make your Wrangler more functional, more organized, and more yours.

1. Tactical Seat Covers That Survive Real Life

Let's start with the single best upgrade most Jeep owners overlook: proper seat covers. Your Wrangler's factory seats take a beating. Trail dust, muddy dogs, spilled coffee, UV damage from running with the top down — it all adds up fast.

The problem with most seat covers is that they look great on day one and start falling apart by month three. Cheap polyester fades. Thin materials tear. Universal-fit covers bunch up and slide around.

That's why Bartact's tactical Jeep Wrangler seat covers are built differently. Each set is made right here in Southern California, using UV-protected polyester combined with 1000D Cordura nylon — the same material used in military-grade gear. The foam and scrim backing gives them a factory-installed feel, and they're designed to fit specific model years so there's no guessing about compatibility.

What really sets them apart is the construction quality you get from domestic manufacturing. Every seat cover is cut, sewn, and inspected by hand. That's a level of quality control you simply can't get from mass-produced imports. They're also Berry Compliant, meaning they meet the standards required for U.S. military procurement — so when we say "military-grade," we actually mean it.

Whether you're running a 2013-18 JK/JKU or a 2018+ JL/JLU, there's a tailored fit waiting for you.

2. MOLLE Panel Organizers: Turn Dead Space Into Storage

One of the most underrated Jeep Wrangler accessories is a MOLLE panel system. If you're not familiar, MOLLE (Modular Lightweight Load-carrying Equipment) is a grid-based attachment system originally designed for military use. It lets you clip pouches, tools, first aid kits, and gear to flat panels using simple webbing straps.

In a Jeep Wrangler, where interior space is always at a premium, MOLLE panels are a game changer. Mount them on your sun visors, behind your seats, or on your tailgate — and suddenly you've got organized, accessible storage where you had nothing before.

Bartact offers a full range of MOLLE accessories and pouches built to the same military spec as their seat covers. We're talking the same 1000D Cordura nylon construction, UV-protected thread, and heavy-duty stitching. These aren't flimsy Amazon pouches that rip the first time you hit a bump on the trail.

Popular setups include a first aid pouch on the driver's sun visor, a multi-tool organizer behind the passenger seat, and phone/radio pouches on the center console. The beauty of MOLLE is that you can rearrange your layout whenever you want — nothing is permanent.

Check out the full MOLLE pouches and gear bags collection to see what's available.

3. Paracord Grab Handles: Style Meets Function

Getting in and out of a lifted Wrangler is an adventure in itself — especially for passengers. Factory grab handles work, but they're boring. And if you've ever tried to haul yourself into a rig with a 3-inch lift using that little plastic nub Jeep gave you, you know the struggle.

Aftermarket paracord grab handles give you a better grip, a custom look, and genuine functionality. Bartact's versions are hand-wrapped with genuine 550 paracord and come in dozens of color combinations so you can match your interior, your mood, or just go full tactical black.

They're available for the A-pillar, the rear soundbar, and the roll bar, so you can outfit the whole cabin. Installation takes about five minutes per handle with no tools required.

Beyond looking great, there's a practical angle here too: in an actual emergency, 550 paracord is rated to hold 550 pounds. That's real survival gear doubling as an interior upgrade.

4. Roll Bar Accessories You Didn't Know You Needed

Your Wrangler's roll bar is prime real estate that most owners completely ignore. Think about it — it's a rigid steel structure running the full length of your cabin. Why not use it?

Roll bar accessories let you mount everything from flashlights and fire extinguishers to storage bags and padding. Bartact's roll bar covers add padding and protection while giving you MOLLE attachment points for even more organization.

A particularly smart setup: pair roll bar covers with a fire extinguisher roll bar mount. If you wheel your Jeep on trails — or honestly, even if you just drive it on the highway — having a fire extinguisher within arm's reach is just smart. The mount keeps it secure, rattle-free, and instantly accessible.

5. Sun Shades with Tactical MOLLE Panels

Running your Wrangler with the top off or the Freedom Panels pulled is half the point of owning one. But the sun can be brutal. Bartact makes sun shades for JL 2-door and JLU 4-door Wranglers that block UV rays while still letting air flow through.

The best part? Several options come with integrated MOLLE panels, so you get shade AND overhead storage. Clip a flashlight, a multi-tool pouch, or your sunglasses case right to the shade above your head. It's the kind of dual-purpose design that makes you wonder why nobody thought of it sooner.

6. Recovery Gear That's Ready When You Need It

No list of Jeep accessories is complete without recovery gear. If you take your Wrangler off-road — even on easy fire roads — you need a basic recovery kit. At minimum: a tow strap, a winch line, and a pair of shackles.

Bartact's recovery gear and winch accessories are built to the same standards as everything else in the line. The patent-pending winch covers deserve special mention — they protect your winch from UV damage, dust, and trail debris while looking clean and purposeful.

Why "Made in USA" Actually Matters for Jeep Accessories

Here's the thing about Jeep accessories: you're bolting them onto a vehicle that goes places most cars never will. Dust, water, extreme heat, vibration, UV exposure — your gear needs to handle all of it.

When Bartact says everything is made in Southern California, that's not just marketing. It means every product is cut and sewn by skilled workers who can see the finished product, catch defects, and maintain consistency that overseas mass production simply can't match.

The materials matter too. UV-protected polyester doesn't fade after a season in the sun. 1000D Cordura nylon doesn't tear when you drag gear across it. Foam and scrim backing doesn't compress into nothing after six months. And Berry Compliance means the entire supply chain — materials and labor — meets U.S. military procurement standards.

Your Jeep is an investment. The accessories you put on it should be too.

Ready to Upgrade?

Browse the full Jeep Wrangler Seat Covers & Accessories collection to see everything available for your specific model year. Whether you're building out a trail rig or just want to protect your daily driver, these are the accessories that'll still look and perform great years from now.

Questions about fitment or recommendations for your specific Wrangler? Reach out to the Bartact team — we're happy to help you find exactly what you need.

articles.title: Best Jeep Wrangler Grab Handles 2026: Roll Bar, A-Pillar & Paracord Options
articles.body_html:
If you've ever hit a technical trail section and watched your passenger white-knuckle the oh-shit handle, you know: factory grab handles don't cut it. Whether you're crawling over boulders in your JL, bombing fire roads in a Gladiator, or sending it in a Bronco, aftermarket grab handles are one of the best upgrades you can make for comfort, safety, and style.

In this guide, we'll break down the best grab handle options for 2026 — from paracord to solid grip — and help you pick the right setup for your rig.

Why Upgrade Your Jeep Grab Handles?

Factory grab handles are typically thin, uncomfortable plastic designed for getting in and out — not for hanging on while your driver sends it through a whoops section. Aftermarket grab handles offer:

  • Better grip surface — wider, textured, or wrapped for all-weather grip
  • Stronger construction — rated for the forces of off-road driving
  • Roll bar mounting — positions handles where you actually need them
  • Style points — because your rig deserves to look as good as it performs

Types of Jeep Grab Handles

Paracord Grab Handles

Paracord grab handles are the go-to for Jeep enthusiasts who want a tactical, custom look. Made from 550-lb rated paracord (the same stuff used by the military), these handles are lightweight, easy to install, and come in dozens of color combinations. They bolt directly to your roll bar using existing mounting points — no drilling required.

Best for: JL/JLU owners who want customization, military/tactical aesthetic, and proven durability. Also the only safe bolt-on grab handles for 2024+ models with roll bar air bags — rigid handles can interfere with airbag deployment, but paracord flexes on impact.

Shop Bartact Paracord Grab Handles →

Solid/Rigid Grab Handles

Solid grab handles — typically made from aluminum, steel, or hard plastic — offer a firm grip point. They're popular for A-pillar mounting and as replacements for factory handles. The trade-off: they can be less forgiving on impacts and may not be compatible with newer roll bar airbag systems.

Ford Bronco Grab Handles

The 2021+ Ford Bronco full-size has its own mounting points and dimensions. Generic Jeep handles won't fit — you need Bronco-specific options. Bartact makes both paracord and plastic grip roll bar handles designed specifically for the Bronco's sport bar.

What to Look for When Buying Grab Handles

  1. Vehicle compatibility — Make sure the handles are designed for your specific model and year. 2024+ Wranglers with roll bar airbags need special consideration.
  2. Mounting method — Bolt-on is best (no drilling). Strap-on handles can slip under load.
  3. Material quality — 550 paracord, marine-grade hardware, UV-resistant materials. Your handles live in the sun and rain.
  4. Made in USA — Quality control matters when you're trusting a handle at 40mph on a washboard road. Bartact manufactures all grab handles in the USA.
  5. Color options — If you're going paracord, you might as well match your interior. Bartact offers 20+ color combinations.

Installation Tips

Most aftermarket grab handles install in under 15 minutes per handle:

  • Remove the factory bolt covers on your roll bar (they pop off)
  • Thread the grab handle mounting bolts through the roll bar holes
  • Torque to spec (usually 25-35 ft-lbs depending on the application)
  • For 2024+ JL/JLU: verify your handles are airbag-compatible before installation

Our Top Picks for 2026

Best Overall: Bartact Paracord Grab Handles (Jeep Wrangler JL/JLU 2018-2026+)

Made in the USA from genuine 550 paracord with stainless steel hardware. Available in 20+ two-tone color combinations. Compatible with all JL and JLU models including 2024+ with roll bar air bags. Sold in pairs.

Shop Paracord Grab Handles →

Best for Ford Bronco: Bartact Bronco Roll Bar Grab Handles

Purpose-built for the 2021+ Ford Bronco full-size sport bar. Available in both paracord (custom colors) and solid plastic grip versions. No drilling — uses factory mounting points.

Shop Bronco Accessories →

Best Budget: Factory Replacement Handles

If you just want to replace a broken factory handle, OEM replacements run about fifteen to twenty-five dollars. They won't perform any better than what came off your Jeep, but they'll get the job done for daily driving.

The Bottom Line

Grab handles are one of the highest bang-for-buck upgrades you can make to your Jeep or Bronco. They improve safety, comfort, and the look of your interior — all for under fifty dollars in most cases. If you're running a 2024+ Wrangler, paracord handles are especially important since they're the only bolt-on option that's safe with the new roll bar airbag system.

Ready to upgrade? Browse Bartact's full grab handle lineup →

articles.title: Best Roll Bar Fire Extinguisher Mounts 2026: Keep Your Rig Safe on Every Trail
articles.body_html:
If you've ever been on a trail when something goes wrong — an electrical short, a fluid leak near hot exhaust, or even a campfire that gets away from you — you know that having a fire extinguisher within arm's reach isn't optional. It's essential.

But here's the problem most off-road enthusiasts face: where do you put a fire extinguisher in a Jeep Wrangler, Ford Bronco, or Toyota Tacoma without it rolling around, taking up valuable cargo space, or being impossible to grab in an emergency?

The answer is a roll bar fire extinguisher mount.

Why You Need a Roll Bar Fire Extinguisher Mount

Tossing a fire extinguisher under your seat or in the trunk isn't good enough. In a real emergency, every second counts. A dedicated roll bar mount solves three critical problems:

  • Instant access — mounted at arm's reach on your roll bar or sport bar, not buried under gear
  • Secure retention — won't rattle loose on rough trails, washboard roads, or rock crawling
  • Space efficiency — frees up floor and cargo space for the gear you actually need on the trail

Most off-road events, trail runs, and organized rides require a mounted fire extinguisher. Even if you never compete, it's one of those upgrades you hope you'll never need — but will be incredibly grateful for if you do.

What to Look for in a Fire Extinguisher Mount

Not all mounts are created equal. Here's what separates a quality mount from a cheap one that'll fail when it matters most:

Material & Construction

Look for mounts made from heavy-duty nylon webbing, Cordura, or reinforced polyester rather than thin metal brackets that can crack, rust, or scratch your roll bar paint. Sewn mounts with bar-tacked stress points are surprisingly strong while being gentler on your vehicle's interior.

MOLLE / PALS Compatibility

A mount with MOLLE (Modular Lightweight Load-carrying Equipment) webbing lets you attach additional pouches, first aid kits, or tools around the extinguisher. This turns a single mount point into a modular safety station. Military-grade PALS webbing is the gold standard here.

Quick Release

In an emergency, you don't want to be fumbling with complicated buckles. The best mounts use a simple velcro or snap system that releases the extinguisher quickly with one hand while keeping it rock-solid during normal driving.

Universal Fit vs. Vehicle-Specific

Roll bar diameters vary between vehicles. A quality mount will either be adjustable or designed specifically for your vehicle's roll bar diameter. Most Jeep Wranglers use a standard roll bar size, but Ford Broncos and other vehicles may differ.

Best Roll Bar Fire Extinguisher Mounts for Off-Road Vehicles

Bartact Roll Bar Fire Extinguisher Holder – EXTREME (MOLLE/PALS Compatible)

The Bartact EXTREME Fire Extinguisher Holder is purpose-built for off-road use. Here's why it stands out:

  • Made in USA (Southern California) — not imported from overseas
  • Full MOLLE/PALS webbing — attach pouches, first aid kits, multi-tools, or flashlights around the extinguisher
  • Heavy-duty UV-protected polyester with 1000D Cordura reinforcement — built to handle desert sun, rain, mud, and years of abuse
  • Foam-backed interior — protects your roll bar finish and prevents rattling
  • Adjustable straps — fits standard roll bar diameters on Jeep Wranglers (JK, JL, TJ), Ford Broncos, Toyota Tacomas, and more
  • Multiple color options — match your interior or go tactical
  • Fits 1 lb fire extinguishers — the most common size for vehicle mounting

→ Shop the Bartact EXTREME Fire Extinguisher Mount

What Size Fire Extinguisher Do You Need?

For most off-road vehicles, a 1 lb or 2.5 lb dry chemical (ABC rated) fire extinguisher is the sweet spot. Here's the breakdown:

  • 1 lb — compact, fits easily on a roll bar, good for electrical fires and small flare-ups. Most common for Jeeps and UTVs.
  • 2.5 lb — more capacity for larger vehicles or if you're running in organized events that require a minimum rating
  • Element E50/E100 — compact aerosol-style extinguishers that are gaining popularity for their small size. Bartact makes magnet mount kits specifically for these.

Pro tip: Check the expiration date on your extinguisher at least once a year. An expired extinguisher is worse than no extinguisher — it gives you false confidence.

Where to Mount Your Fire Extinguisher

The best mounting location depends on your vehicle:

  • Jeep Wrangler (TJ/JK/JL): The sport bar directly behind the driver's seat is the most popular spot. You can reach it without unbuckling. The passenger side sport bar works too, especially if you have a co-driver.
  • Ford Bronco: The rear roll bar hoop gives easy access from either front seat. Some owners mount on the B-pillar area.
  • Toyota Tacoma: Behind the driver's seat on the roll bar or on a bed-mounted cage if you have one.
  • UTV/Side-by-Side: Center of the roll cage, within reach of both driver and passenger.

Fire Extinguisher Mount vs. Floor Bracket: Which Is Better?

Floor brackets bolt to your tub or bed, while roll bar mounts strap to existing bars. Here's the honest comparison:

Feature Roll Bar Mount Floor Bracket
Accessibility ✅ Arm's reach ❌ Have to reach down
Installation ✅ No drilling ❌ Requires bolts/drilling
Space ✅ Uses dead space ❌ Takes floor space
Rattle-free ✅ With foam backing ⚠️ Metal on metal
Removability ✅ Easy on/off ❌ Semi-permanent

Roll bar mounts win on almost every metric. The only advantage of floor brackets is they can sometimes hold larger (5+ lb) extinguishers that would be too heavy for a strap mount.

MOLLE Accessories to Pair with Your Fire Extinguisher Mount

If you choose a MOLLE-compatible mount like the Bartact EXTREME, you can build out a complete safety station on your roll bar:

  • MOLLE pouches — carry a first aid kit, flashlight, or multi-tool right next to your extinguisher
  • Medical pouches — IFAK (Individual First Aid Kit) pouch for serious trail medical supplies
  • Utility straps — secure additional gear to the MOLLE webbing

The modular approach means you're not just mounting a fire extinguisher — you're building a trail-ready safety system.

Final Thoughts

A roll bar fire extinguisher mount is one of those upgrades that costs relatively little but could save your vehicle — or your life. Whether you're running trails every weekend or just want peace of mind for your daily driver, having a properly mounted, easily accessible fire extinguisher is a no-brainer.

Choose a mount that's built to last, easy to install, and keeps the extinguisher within arm's reach. And if you want MOLLE compatibility for a full safety setup, the Bartact EXTREME is about as good as it gets — Made in USA, built with the same materials the military trusts, and designed specifically for off-road abuse.

→ Shop All Bartact Fire Extinguisher Mounts & Safety Gear

articles.title: Best Winch Covers for Warn Winches: Why Your Winch Needs Protection
articles.body_html:
You spent good money on your Warn winch. It pulls you out of mud pits, hauls you up rocky inclines, and gives you the confidence to push your rig further than you probably should. But here's the thing most people overlook: that winch is sitting out in the open, taking a beating from the elements every single day — whether you're on the trail or parked in your driveway.

UV rays, rain, dust, mud, salt spray, bird droppings — your winch soaks it all up. And over time, that punishment degrades seals, corrodes electrical connections, cracks synthetic rope, and turns your investment into a liability. A quality winch cover is the simplest, cheapest insurance policy you can buy for your recovery setup.

Why You Need a Winch Cover

If your winch lives on the front of your Jeep Wrangler, Ford Bronco, Toyota Tacoma, or any other trail rig, it's exposed to everything nature throws at it — plus everything your tires kick up. Here's what happens without protection:

  • UV damage to synthetic winch rope — Synthetic lines degrade in sunlight. A damaged rope can snap under load, and that's not just an inconvenience — it's a safety hazard.
  • Corrosion of electrical components — Solenoids, contacts, and wiring corrode when exposed to moisture and salt, leading to intermittent failures or a dead winch when you need it most.
  • Seal degradation — Rubber seals dry out and crack from UV exposure, allowing water and debris to infiltrate the motor housing.
  • Cosmetic damage — Fading, oxidation, and grime buildup make your winch look neglected, dragging down the whole front end of your build.

Bottom line: a $30–$70 cover can protect a $500–$1,500 winch. That math checks out every time.

What Makes a Great Winch Cover?

Not all winch covers are created equal. The cheap neoprene sleeves and generic nylon bags you see on Amazon might technically "cover" your winch, but they come with serious compromises:

  • Poor fit — Universal covers bunch up, flap in the wind, and leave gaps that let moisture and debris in.
  • Flimsy materials — Thin nylon and vinyl covers fade, crack, and disintegrate within a season or two of UV exposure.
  • Removal required for use — Most covers need to be completely removed before you can use your winch, which costs precious time on the trail.

What you actually want is a cover that's model-specific for a precision fit, built from materials that can handle years of sun and weather, and ideally one you don't have to remove every time you need your winch.

Bartact Winch Covers: Patent-Pending Design, Built in the USA

At Bartact, we designed our winch covers the same way we design everything — by identifying what was wrong with existing products and engineering a better solution from scratch.

Our patent-pending winch covers are made right here in Southern California, using the same UV-protected polyester fabric and foam backing we use in our Jeep Wrangler seat covers. This isn't some afterthought product — it's built to the same quality standard that our seat covers are known for.

Key Features

  • No-removal design (patent pending) — A front zippered opening lets you spool your winch line in and out without removing the cover. When it's time to winch, unzip and go. When you're done, zip it back up. No fumbling with snaps, no stuffing a loose cover behind your bumper.
  • Model-specific fit — These aren't one-size-fits-all. Each cover is tailored to a specific Warn winch model, so it fits like a glove with no excess material flapping around.
  • UV-protected fabric with foam backing — The same material that protects interiors from the sun protects your winch from the same punishment. The 1/4" open-cell foam backing provides cushioning and additional insulation.
  • Protects your rope — Synthetic winch rope is especially vulnerable to UV degradation. The cover shields your entire fairlead and rope area from direct sun exposure.
  • Two color options — Available in Black and Graphite to match your rig.

Compatible Warn Winch Models

Bartact currently offers covers for the most popular Warn winch series:

  • Warn Zeon 10 & 12 Series — One of the most popular winch lines on the market, found on everything from Jeep Wranglers to built Tacomas.
  • Warn VR EVO / New Gen VR 10 & 12 — Warn's updated VR line with improved motors and controls. These covers are specifically sized for the new-generation housing.
  • Warn XD9, M8, 9.5XP & Gen I VR — Covers the older but still widely used Warn models. If you've been running one of these tried-and-true winches for years, it definitely deserves some protection by now.

All three options are priced at $69.99 — a fraction of what a winch replacement or rebuild costs.

Winch Cover vs. Winch Maintenance: Do You Need Both?

A cover doesn't replace maintenance, but it dramatically reduces how often you need to do it. Think of it this way:

Without Cover With Cover
Monthly cleaning of debris and buildup Quick wipe-down every few months
Frequent rope inspection for UV damage Rope stays protected between uses
Annual seal and electrical checks Seals last longer with UV protection
Potential early winch failure Extended winch lifespan

You should still inspect your winch periodically and service it according to the manufacturer's recommendations. But a cover means those inspections are more about peace of mind and less about damage control.

Pair Your Winch Cover with the Right Recovery Gear

While you're thinking about protecting your winch setup, it's worth making sure the rest of your recovery kit is dialed in too. Bartact and Bull Strap offer a full lineup of recovery and winch accessories:

  • D-Ring Shackles — Our Bull Strap shackles are rated up to 72,000 lbs (8T heavy duty) and come with isolators and washers to reduce rattle.
  • Winch Straps — Heavy-duty recovery straps built to handle real loads.
  • Cargo Tie-Downs — Secure your gear so it's not flying around when the trail gets rough.
  • Fire Extinguisher Mounts — MOLLE-compatible roll bar mounts for quick-access fire safety on the trail.

The Bottom Line

Your winch is one of the most important — and most expensive — accessories on your rig. Leaving it unprotected is like buying a $2,000 set of off-road lights and never washing the lenses. It'll still work for a while, but you're shortening its life and asking for trouble.

Bartact's patent-pending winch covers give your Warn winch a custom-fit shield against UV, moisture, dust, and debris — and you don't even have to remove them to use your winch. At $69.99, it's the easiest decision in your entire build.

Browse Bartact Winch Covers →

articles.title: Best Ford Bronco Seat Covers 2026: Keep Your 6th Gen Looking New
articles.body_html:
The Ford Bronco is back — and if you're using yours the way it was meant to be used, your seats are taking a beating. Trail mud, sand, rain, sunscreen, dog hair — the 6th Gen Bronco's interior wasn't built for a clean room. It was built for adventure. Your seat covers should be too.

Whether you've got a 2-door or 4-door, Base or Badlands, soft top or hard top — seat covers are the single best investment you can make to protect your Bronco's resale value and keep the cabin looking factory-fresh.

Why the Bronco Specifically Needs Seat Covers

The Bronco has some unique characteristics that make seat protection especially important:

  • Removable doors and top — when the doors come off, your seats are fully exposed to the elements. Rain, UV, dust — everything hits them directly.
  • Trail use — the Bronco attracts owners who actually go off-road. That means mud, water crossings, and dirty gear on the seats constantly.
  • Resale value — Broncos hold their value exceptionally well. Stained, faded, or worn seats will cost you thousands at trade-in time.
  • Light-colored factory upholstery — many Bronco trims come with lighter interior colors that show every stain.

What Makes a Great Bronco Seat Cover

Custom Fit Is Essential

The Bronco's seats have a unique shape — especially the bolsters on the Badlands and Wildtrak trims. Universal covers will slip, bunch, and look terrible. You need covers patterned specifically for your year and trim.

Material Requirements

For a vehicle that spends time with no doors and no top, your seat cover material needs to deliver:

  • UV protection — with the top off, seats get direct sunlight for hours
  • Water resistance — rain happens. Water crossings happen. Wet bathing suits happen.
  • Abrasion resistance — trail gear, tools, and recovery equipment sliding across seats
  • Color retention — cheap materials fade fast under direct sun exposure

Airbag Compatibility

The Bronco has seat-mounted side airbags. Your covers must have properly sewn SRS breakaway seams. This isn't optional — it's a safety requirement.

Bartact Ford Bronco Seat Covers

Every Bartact Bronco seat cover set is custom-patterned for the 2021+ Ford Bronco Full-Size (2-door and 4-door). Made in Southern California, these aren't mass-produced overseas imports with a "custom fit" label slapped on.

What's Included

  • Front seat covers (driver and passenger) — each patterned for the specific seat, not mirrored
  • Rear seat covers (60/40 split bench on 4-door models)
  • Headrest covers included
  • All mounting hardware and installation guides

Construction Details

  • Primary material: High-grade UV-protected polyester — engineered to resist fading even with the top removed
  • Reinforcement: 1000D Cordura nylon in high-wear zones (bolsters, edges, adjustment areas)
  • Backing: Foam and scrim backing protects factory upholstery and adds comfort
  • Stitching: Double and triple stitching with bar-tacked seams at every stress point
  • Airbag compatible: SRS-compliant breakaway seams tested for proper deployment

Color Options for Your Bronco

Bartact offers multiple color combinations so you can match your Bronco's personality — from all-black tactical to two-tone combinations that complement the Bronco's rugged styling. Popular choices include:

  • All Black — clean, tactical, hides everything
  • Black/Graphite — subtle contrast, factory-upgrade look
  • Black/Coyote — pairs perfectly with the Bronco's available tan interior
  • Black/ACU Camo — for the serious trail rider

Installation: Easier Than You Think

Most Bronco owners complete a full install in about 45 minutes to an hour. No tools required. The covers slip over your existing seats and secure underneath with straps and hooks. Key tips:

  1. Start with the front seats — remove the headrests first
  2. Pull the cover over the seatback, then tuck and secure
  3. Cover the seat bottom — tuck the front edge first, then sides
  4. Use the tuck flap in the seat crease for a wrinkle-free finish
  5. Move to the rear — the 60/40 split makes rear installation straightforward

Complete Your Bronco's Interior Protection

  • MOLLE panels and door bags — organize gear on the back of seats and inside doors

articles.title: Best Toyota 4Runner Seat Covers 2026: Protect Your 5th Gen Interior
articles.body_html:
The Toyota 4Runner is built to go anywhere — but that means your interior takes a beating. Between trail dust, muddy boots, wet dogs, and years of daily driving, the factory upholstery on your 5th Gen 4Runner (2010–2024) doesn't stand a chance without protection.

The right seat covers protect your investment, add comfort, and can completely transform the look of your cabin. But with so many options out there — from cheap universal-fit covers to premium custom solutions — how do you choose?

What to Look for in 4Runner Seat Covers

Before comparing brands, here's what actually matters when choosing seat covers for your 4Runner:

Custom Fit vs. Universal Fit

Universal seat covers are tempting because they're cheap. But on a 4Runner, they bunch up around the headrests, slip off the bolsters during hard cornering on trails, and never look right. Custom-fit covers are pattern-cut specifically for your year and trim — they fit like a glove, stay put, and look factory-installed.

Material Matters

Your 4Runner's seat covers need to handle everything from beach trips to mountain trails. Look for:

  • UV protection — essential if you live somewhere sunny (the 4Runner's windows let in a lot of light)
  • Water resistance — for post-trail cleanups and rainy day adventures
  • Durability — heavy-duty materials that won't wear through in high-friction areas like the bolsters
  • Breathability — nobody wants to slide around on plastic-feeling covers in July

Airbag Compatibility

This is non-negotiable. Your 4Runner has side-impact airbags built into the front seats. Any seat cover you install must be SRS airbag compatible with properly sewn breakaway seams. Covers that block airbag deployment are a safety hazard — don't risk it.

Why Bartact for Your 4Runner

Bartact seat covers are designed and manufactured in Southern California. Every set for the Toyota 4Runner (2010–2024) is custom-patterned, not generic. Here's what sets them apart:

  • Made in the USA — designed, cut, and sewn in California
  • High-grade UV-protected polyester as the primary fabric — tough, fade-resistant, and comfortable
  • 1000D Cordura nylon reinforcement in high-wear areas for maximum durability
  • Foam and scrim backing — adds comfort while protecting your factory upholstery underneath
  • Double and triple stitching with bar-tacked stress points
  • SRS airbag compatible — properly engineered breakaway seams
  • Multiple color combinations — match your style, not just your vehicle

4Runner-Specific Fitment

Bartact covers for the 5th Gen 4Runner account for every detail of your specific seats:

  • Proper cutouts for seat adjusters and controls
  • Headrest cover included (not sold separately like some brands)
  • Center console armrest cover available
  • Compatible with both SR5 and TRD/Limited trim seats
  • Easy installation — no tools required, most installs take under an hour

4Runner Seat Cover Installation Tips

Installing seat covers on a 4Runner is straightforward, but a few tips will help you get the best result:

  1. Remove headrests first — makes pulling the cover over the seatback much easier
  2. Start with the bottom cushion — tuck the front edge under first, then secure the sides and rear
  3. Use the tuck-in flap — Bartact covers have a flap that tucks into the seat crease for a tight, wrinkle-free fit
  4. Work in sections — don't try to pull everything tight at once. Get one area seated, then move to the next
  5. Reinstall headrests last — push them through the cover's headrest holes firmly until they click

Beyond Seat Covers: Protecting Your Entire 4Runner Interior

Once your seats are protected, consider these upgrades for the rest of your cabin:

  • MOLLE seat back panels — organize gear, tools, and accessories on the back of your front seats
  • Grab handles — paracord grab handles for passengers on rough trails
  • Cargo solutions — keep your trunk area organized with modular storage

Your 4Runner was built to last 300,000+ miles. Protect the interior so it still looks great when you get there.
